formula: If + Subject + had + Past Participle, Subject + would have + Past Participle
formula_visual: If + [IMPOSSIBLE PAST CONDITION, Past Perfect], + [IMAGINED PAST RESULT, would have + past participle]
when_to_use: Use the Third Conditional to talk about an IMPOSSIBLE, unchangeable past situation — imagining how a different past action would have led to a different past result. Both the condition and its result are already finished and cannot be changed; this is purely imaginative regret, relief, or reflection.
when_to_use_ar: استخدم الشرط الثالث للحديث عن موقف مستحيل في الماضي ما ممكن تغييره — تخيل إن فعل ماضٍ مختلف كان يؤدي لنتيجة ماضية مختلفة. الشرط والنتيجة الاتنين خلصوا وما ممكن تغييرهم.
why_arabic_speakers_struggle: This is grammatically the most complex conditional (Past Perfect + would have + past participle), and learners often either simplify it into Second Conditional forms (losing the 'impossible past' meaning) or make participle errors, since it requires stacking two different perfect-aspect forms correctly in one sentence.

examples.en: If I had studied harder, I would have passed the exam.
examples.ar: لو كنت ذاكرت أكتر، كنت نجحت في الامتحان (الامتحان خلص، محصلش)
examples.structure: [If] + [I had studied harder] + [I would have passed the exam]

examples.en: She wouldn't have missed the flight if she had left earlier.
examples.ar: كانت ما فوتتش الطيارة لو كانت خرجت بدري
examples.structure: [She wouldn't have missed the flight] + [if] + [she had left earlier]

examples.en: If they had known about the traffic, they would have taken a different route.
examples.ar: لو كانوا عارفين عن الزحمة، كانوا اخدوا طريق تاني
examples.structure: [If] + [they had known about the traffic] + [they would have taken a different route]

examples.en: I would have called you if I had had your number.
examples.ar: كنت اتصلت بيك لو كان عندي رقمك
examples.structure: [I would have called you] + [if] + [I had had your number]

examples.en: What would you have done if you had been in my position?
examples.ar: كنت هتعمل إيه لو كنت في موقفي؟ (سؤال افتراضي عن الماضي)
examples.structure: [What would you have done] + [if] + [you had been in my position]

common_errors.wrong: If I studied harder, I would have passed.
common_errors.correct: If I had studied harder, I would have passed.
common_errors.explanation: The if-clause needs Past Perfect ('had studied'), not Past Simple — Past Simple alone signals Second Conditional (unreal present), not Third Conditional (impossible past).

common_errors.wrong: If I had studied harder, I would pass.
common_errors.correct: If I had studied harder, I would have passed.
common_errors.explanation: The result clause needs 'would have + past participle', not just 'would + base verb' — that combination belongs to Second Conditional instead.
After 'would have', the verb must be a past participle ('called'), not the base form ('call').

practice_fill_blank.sentence: If she ___ (arrive) on time, she ___ (not/miss) the meeting.
practice_fill_blank.hint: Impossible past condition + imagined past result.

practice_fill_blank.sentence: We ___ (win) the game if we ___ (practice) more.
practice_fill_blank.hint: Result clause first this time — same rule applies.

practice_fill_blank.sentence: If I ___ (know) about the sale, I ___ (buy) more.
practice_fill_blank.hint: Regret about a missed past opportunity.

practice_fill_blank.sentence: He ___ (be) upset if he ___ (find out) the truth.
practice_fill_blank.answer: would have been / had found out

practice_fill_blank.sentence: If they ___ (not/help) us, we ___ (fail) the project.

quick_rule: Third Conditional = IMPOSSIBLE past, already finished, can't be changed. If-clause: had + past participle (Past Perfect). Result clause: would have + past participle. This is regret/reflection about the unchangeable past, not a real possibility.
quick_rule_ar: الشرط الثالث = ماضٍ مستحيل، انتهى، وما ممكن تغييره. جملة if: had + التصريف الثالث. جملة النتيجة: would have + التصريف الثالث.
mnemonic: THIRD Conditional = the THIRD time you replay the past in your head, wishing it went differently — but it's over.
connect_to_speaking: Practice with your buddy: share one thing you regret NOT doing and one thing you're glad you DID do, using Third Conditional for the regret ('If I had...') and explaining the real outcome afterward.
